title: Tapping for the No
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the no you need to say and have been carrying for days
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the no you have been holding for days. The script settles the body around the no so it can leave your mouth without a long apology trail.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping shape is EFT. EFT was made by Gary Craig in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use before sending the cancel message, before the meeting where the no needs to be said, or in the days the no has been sitting in your chest and not yet leaving your mouth.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the no you have been carrying. The body has been bracing for the conversation for days. The script settles it around the no so the no can leave your mouth.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I have been carrying this no for days and have not said it yet, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am afraid of letting them down, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though saying no feels harder than living through the yes, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I have a no I need to say.
**Side of eye:** I have been carrying it for days.
**Under eye:** I am worried about what they will think.
**Under nose:** I am tired of saying yes when I mean no.
**Chin:** My body has been telling me this is too much.
**Collarbone:** I have not been listening.
**Under arm:** The yes I gave was not really mine.
**Top of head:** Holding the no is heavier than saying it.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I can say the no I need to say.
**Side of eye:** The waiting has been heavier than the saying will be.
**Under eye:** Their reading of me is not my business.
**Under nose:** I am allowed to say no without a long explanation.
**Chin:** My body has good information.
**Collarbone:** I am listening now.
**Under arm:** A clean no makes room for the real yes.
**Top of head:** I can let the no leave my mouth today.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping shape is EFT. EFT was made by Gary Craig in the mid-1990s.
